full_text_rationale: Full text proposes PhyloEvolve, a GPU code-optimization agent system with persistent phylogenetic trees and an elite pool that store trajectories, high-performing variants, reusable modifications, and failure patterns for cross-lineage reuse across scientific-code optimization tasks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 010; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Leyi Zhao, Weijie Huang, Yitong Guo, Jiang Bian, Chenghong Wang, Xuhong Zhang
titulo: Large Language Model-Powered Evolutionary Code Optimization on a Phylogenetic Tree
formato_armazenamento: Persistent phylogenetic forest whose nodes store algorithm versions, code, performance metrics, modification history, and rewards; global Elite Pool stores elite trajectories and reusable elite modifications with performance statistics.
framework_agente: PhyloEvolve multi-agent GPU scientific-code optimization framework with NextStepper, ModifyAgent, Designer, and Summarizer agents
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; controlled sandbox execution; trajectory-conditioned evolutionary optimization; qualitative and quantitative performance analysis
benchmarks_utilizados: Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert equation solver, Local Tangent Space Alignment manifold learning, GraphWave spectral graph embedding; NVIDIA A40 workstation with Docker CUDA execution
metricas_desempenho: wall-clock runtime, memory efficiency, correctness success/failure signal, scalar performance score, trajectory rewards, convergence behavior
metricas_cl: Elite trajectory reuse, elite modification catalog, cross-lineage transfer, pruning with informative failure retention, summarization of successful patterns
baseline_comparado: Baseline implementations and evolutionary search variants for the scientific-code optimization workloads
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Phylogenetic trees and a forest-level elite pool grow across optimization iterations, preserving complete or partial high-performing trajectories, modification histories, rewards, and reusable optimization motifs.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; experiments report GPU hardware, Docker execution, timeouts, memory limits, checkpointing, pruning, and up to three automatic debug retries.
resultado_principal: PhyloEvolve reports consistent performance-enhancing transformations across PDE solving, manifold learning, and spectral graph workloads by reusing optimization trajectories rather than relying only on mutation-driven evolutionary search.
desempenho_vs_baseline: The paper reports improvements in runtime performance, memory efficiency, and correctness over baseline and evolutionary methods, including reusable motifs such as kernel fusion, shared-memory tiling, and batching strategies.
custo_vs_baseline: No monetary or token-cost comparison is reported; the discussion emphasizes that trajectory-conditioned reuse reduces redundant exploration but still depends on empirical evaluation.
limitacoes: The system lacks hardware-aware cost models, can be inefficient for computationally heavy workloads, focuses on NVIDIA GPUs, does not address multi-GPU or distributed execution, and may produce occasional low-quality LLM-generated modifications.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on empirical runtime measurement, hardware/backend choice, benchmark workload selection, container execution, pruning settings, LLM proposal quality, and the absence of a theoretical account of when trajectory reuse yields stable improvement operators.
